commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Montelukast (Singulair) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Publix pharmacies ubiquitous. Publix offers select generic medications free (antibiotics, lisinopril, metformin). Mail-order through Part D plans available.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Florida has a large Medicare Advantage market with many $0-premium plans that include drug coverage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions. Florida's large retiree population means highly competitive Medicare Advantage market with generous drug benefits. Many plans offer $0 copay on tier 1 generics.

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Many FL Medicare Advantage plans include comprehensive vision benefits with $0-25 exam copay and $100-250 eyewear allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-2 weeks for routine eye exams. Florida has a high density of optometrists and ophthalmologists due to retiree population. Shorter waits than national average.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. Florida ophthalmologists experienced with age-related conditions.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Many FL Medicare Advantage plans include dental with $0 preventive copay. Standalone plans (Delta Dental, Humana) popular.
dental.dentistAvailability: Excellent. Florida's large retiree population supports a high density of dental providers. Many practices cater specifically to seniors. Wait times typically 1-2 weeks.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Early bird specials common in FL. AARP restaurant discounts widely accepted.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$32 + 6% tax + 20% tip = ~$40/meal, 2x/week = ~$330/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: AT&T and Xfinity offer senior/AARP discounts on select plans.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: FL state parks senior discount. National parks Senior Pass.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at major chains.

monthlyPass.notes: LYNX bus (Orlando) senior monthly pass $25. SunRail monthly pass $79.50 (no specific senior pass). Varies by metro area.

publicTransit.coverage: Limited. LYNX bus in Orlando, SunRail commuter rail (Orlando), Brightline (Miami-Orlando-West Palm Beach). Most FL metro areas have basic bus service only. Very car-dependent state.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Most FL transit agencies offer 50% discount for riders 65+ with Medicare card. LYNX half-fare $1.00. Miami-Dade Metrorail half-fare for seniors.

rideShare.availability: Good in metro areas (Orlando, Tampa, Miami, Fort Lauderdale). Limited in smaller cities and rural areas.
rideShare.notes: GoGoGrandparent available for seniors preferring phone-based ride ordering. Some FL counties offer subsidized senior transportation services.

registration.notes: Florida initial registration includes title fee ($77.25), plate fee ($28), registration ($46.15-56.15), and local fees. No personal property tax on vehicles. Annual renewal ~$75.

insurance.notes: Florida car insurance moderate. No-fault state requiring PIP coverage. Senior safe driver discounts available (5-15%). Rates vary significantly by county.

fuel.notes: ~$3.40/gallon average in Florida. FL gas tax $0.362/gallon (combined state/local). Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Free parking common in suburban areas. Downtown areas in Miami/Tampa charge $2-4/hour. Most apartment complexes include parking.

tolls.notes: Florida has extensive toll roads. SunPass transponder required for many highways. Florida's Turnpike, I-4 Express, 417, 429, Sawgrass Expressway. Can add $40-100/month depending on driving patterns.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Florida Mature Driver course (AARP Smart Driver) gives 5-15% insurance discount. Some counties offer free or subsidized senior transport (dial-a-ride programs).

transportation.walkability: Low in most areas. Walkable pockets in downtown Miami, St. Petersburg, Winter Park. Most Florida development is suburban and car-dependent.

cycling.infrastructure: Improving. Some bike lanes in urban areas. Pinellas Trail, trails along coast. Heat and rain limit cycling 4-5 months/year.
cycling.notes: CitiBike Miami $5/ride or $17/month. Tampa Bay bike share available. Infrastructure varies widely by city.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month. Florida has one of the most competitive Medicare Advantage markets.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: No State Income Tax
discounts.description: Florida has no state income tax. All retirement income (SS, pensions, 401k withdrawals, IRA distributions) is tax-free at the state level.

discounts.name: Florida Senior Transit Discounts
discounts.description: Most FL transit agencies offer 50% fare reduction for riders 65+. Present Medicare card or state ID as proof.

discounts.name: Florida Homestead Exemption
discounts.description: Primary residence exemption reduces assessed property value by $50,000. Additional $50,000 exemption for seniors 65+ with household income under $36,614.

discounts.name: SHINE Medicare Counseling
discounts.description: Free Medicare counseling service for Florida seniors. Help choosing plans, understanding benefits, and resolving billing issues.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year).

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. FL wireless tax ~13% + Communications Services Tax. 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: Florida residential rent exempt from sales tax since June 2023 (was previously taxed at 5.5% + county surtax). No state income tax. Property tax is landlord responsibility. Higher renter's insurance due to hurricane risk.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. No statutory limit on security deposit. Landlord must hold deposit in separate account. Florida Residential Landlord and Tenant Act applies.
housing.marketNotes: Market varies significantly: Tampa/St. Pete $1,800-2,400, Orlando $1,700-2,300, Jacksonville $1,500-2,000, Miami $2,500+. Prices surged 2021-2023, now stabilizing. Hurricane insurance costs push up rents.

sources.name: FL Dept of Revenue - Sales Tax on Rent

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: South Florida has an excellent selection of Black hair salons. Central FL also well-served.

providers.name: Curl Envy Natural Hair Studio
providers.notes: Specializes in natural hair, locs, twist-outs, color on natural hair. Black-owned.

providers.name: Tresses Hair Studio
providers.notes: Color, relaxers, protective styles, silk press. Experienced with all textures.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Plentiful options statewide. Many salons cater to retirees with senior discount days.

providers.name: Salon Lofts
providers.notes: Individual stylists in private suites. Color specialists available. Competitive pricing.

providers.name: Aveda Salon & Spa
providers.notes: Plant-based color products, gentle on aging hair. Highlights, balayage, grey blending.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL stores in most metro areas. Burlington and Ross carry some big sizes at discount prices.

providers.name: DXL Big + Tall
providers.notes: Primary big & tall destination. Lightweight fabrics suited for FL climate.

providers.name: Bealls / Belk
providers.notes: Southern department stores with extended size sections. Good for casual and resort wear.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Major malls and outlet centers throughout FL. Climate-appropriate clothing year-round.

providers.name: Chico's
providers.notes: FL-based retailer. Sizes 000-4 (equiv. 0-20). Great for travel-friendly, wrinkle-free clothing.

providers.name: Macy's / Dillard's
providers.notes: Full regular-size range. Good sales and loyalty programs. Alterations available.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Shoe shopping is easy in FL. Open-toe and sandal-friendly climate means more options.

providers.name: DSW
providers.notes: Wide width selection. Good clearance. Comfort brands like Clarks, ECCO, Vionic.

providers.name: Famous Footwear
providers.notes: Mid-range prices. Frequent BOGO sales. Athletic and casual shoes in extended sizes.
